metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the FP-discrimination guard conditions (parallel to the 8 recall patterns) so an agent can write and a reviewer can verify the complete overlay.
findings.finding: The FP-discrimination guards ('both halves') are never enumerated; an executing agent knows the 8 recall patterns explicitly but has no corresponding list of FP-guard conditions to implement, leaving the content of half the overlay undefined.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a numbered list of FP-guard patterns analogous to the recall list, e.g.: (1) pure-CPU single-call (no loop/accumulation), (2) bounded/constant-size collection, (3) already-async/non-blocking context, (4) explicit cache/memoization present, (5) result discarded immediately (no accumulation path), etc.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `eval_solver.run_case()` (or an alternative runner path) can execute `code-review-performance` as a prompt ID before adding the eval spec, or explicitly budget the solver extension as part of this story's scope.
findings.finding: The plan's second deliverable — 'eval spec + fixtures for the performance overlay' — would be the first-ever code-review-*.eval.yaml in the codebase (eval_specs/ contains only plan-review and non-code-review evals), and eval_solver.run_case() has no execution arm for code-review overlay prompt IDs (it raises ValueError for any unknown non-criterion prompt_id, line 171). This makes the eval spec a 0-call-site, unexecutable artifact under the current infrastructure — a premature abstraction by the Rule-of-Three standard.
findings.location: Files section / AC2: 'eval spec + fixtures for the performance overlay'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and cite the concrete codebase/doc path (or external artifact URL) that constitutes 'dso content', so the 8 recall patterns and FP-guard vocabulary have a verifiable authoritative source.
findings.finding: The plan's entire enrichment is predicated on 'dso content' as the authoritative source for both the 8 recall patterns and the FP-guards, but 'dso' yields zero matches in `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/` and 'FP-guard' yields zero matches there; neither 'breaks-under-load' nor 'worse-with-scale' (the plan's named bright-line reasoning) appear anywhere in the codebase. The source artifact that defines this content is unverifiable.
findings.location: What section — 'Enrich the performance overlay prompt with dso content'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (or add) the eval execution path for `code-review-performance`: either extend `eval_solver.run_case()` with a code-review overlay arm, or name the alternative harness that can run recall + no-fire fixtures against this prompt id.
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval', implying an eval execution path exists for the `code-review-performance` prompt id. However, `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` contains no `code-review-performance.eval.yaml`, and `eval_solver.run_case()` raises `ValueError` for any non-criterion, non-completion-verifier/ticket-quality/spec-alignment prompt id — there is no solver arm for code-review overlay prompts.
findings.location: AC2 — 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the eval_solver.py change (or alternative harness mechanism) needed to execute the code-review-performance overlay eval, or explicitly acknowledge that the eval spec will use a different execution path that already supports this prompt type.
findings.finding: eval_solver.run_case() has no execution arm for the prompt_id 'code-review-performance' (or any code-review-* overlay). The solver handles only completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment, and plan-review criteria; any other id hits `raise ValueError(f"no eval solver for prompt {prompt_id!r}")` at eval_solver.py:171. AC-2's 'passing recall + no-fire eval' cannot run without either extending eval_solver or using a different harness path — neither is mentioned in the plan.
findings.location: AC-2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'; src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py:106-171

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the eval harness will execute `code-review-performance` recall/no-fire cases — either by extending `eval_solver.run_case()` with a code-review overlay arm (feeding a synthetic diff as `ticket_context`) or by adopting a different eval execution path — and confirm the mechanism before writing fixtures.
findings.finding: The eval mechanism asserted by AC2 ('Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval') cannot execute with the current harness: eval_solver.run_case() has no execution arm for the 'code-review-performance' prompt_id (it handles only completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment, and plan-review criteria); calling it with 'code-review-performance' raises ValueError. No code-review-performance.eval.yaml exists in src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/. The plan does not describe how to extend the eval harness to support code-review overlay prompt IDs.
findings.location: AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `code-review-performance` arm to eval_solver.run_case() (or a new `code_review_overlay_solver`) that seeds a disposable diff fixture and calls the performance overlay via the real op, analogous to how the existing arms scaffold a ticket store. The eval spec's `case` shape would carry a `diff_text` and a `files` map; the arm assembles the context and invokes the overlay's agentic op.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'dso content' source for FP-guards: either enumerate the FP-discrimination guard conditions inline in the plan (as the recall patterns are), or cite the exact file path / section in the repo where this content lives.
findings.finding: The plan attributes both the 8 recall patterns and the FP-guards to 'dso content' as their source, but 'dso' does not appear anywhere in the repo as a named content artifact or document carrying performance review criteria — leaving the FP-guard half of AC1 with no resolvable source an executor can look up.
findings.location: What section: 'Enrich the performance overlay prompt with dso content: recall patterns AND FP-guards (both halves)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'FP-guards' subsection to the What or Files section that lists the guard conditions explicitly (e.g., 'do not flag a cache read inside a loop if the cache is a local in-memory dict; do not flag async I/O that is explicitly awaited in a non-blocking context'), mirroring the inline enumeration already provided for the 8 recall patterns.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify how the `code-review-performance` eval will be executed: either extend `eval_solver.run_case` with a diff-fixture arm for agentic overlays, or explicitly name the alternative runner path — without this, AC2's passing-eval gate cannot be verified.
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' for the `code-review-performance` overlay, but `eval_solver.run_case` has no execution arm for `code-review-performance` — it only handles `completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, `spec-alignment`, and plan-review criterion ids. Running a code-reading agentic overlay eval requires a disposable diff/repo fixture and a different invocation path that does not currently exist in the harness.
findings.location: AC2 — 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'; src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py lines 136–171

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name or locate the 'dso content' source explicitly (file path, URL, or inline definition) so that reviewers can verify the recall patterns and FP-guards were drawn from the stated authoritative source.
findings.finding: The plan states 'Enrich the performance overlay prompt with dso content' as the mechanism for both recall patterns and FP-guards, but 'dso' has no traceable definition or location in the codebase (`src/rebar` search returns zero semantic matches for 'dso' as a content source; occurrences are only eval corpus labels). It is unclear whether 'dso' names an external document, an internal artifact, or a working shorthand — leaving the source of the 8 recall patterns and FP-guards unresolved.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Enrich the performance overlay prompt with dso content'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the FP-guard value vocabulary in the plan: enumerate each guard condition (the 'both halves'), what pattern it suppresses, and why — or cite the exact document/section where these are already specified.
findings.finding: The FP-guard value vocabulary ('both halves') is referenced in the plan but never enumerated anywhere — an executor cannot implement FP-guards whose conditions, trigger logic, or discrimination criteria are unspecified.
findings.location: Plan § What: 'FP-guards (both halves)' / AC1: 'performance overlay carries both recall patterns and FP-discrimination guards'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify 'dso content' precisely: provide the file path, ticket ID, or URL where the 8 recall patterns and FP-guards live, or inline the content directly in the plan.
findings.finding: 'dso content' is cited as the authoritative source for both the 8 recall patterns and the FP-guards, but no 'dso' document, module, or schema exists anywhere in src/rebar/llm/ — the provenance is unresolvable.
findings.location: Plan § What: 'Enrich the performance overlay prompt with dso content'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'passing recall + no-fire eval' with a numeric threshold (e.g., ≥7/8 recall patterns triggered, 0 false positives on N defined no-fire fixtures) so the criterion is objectively evaluable in-session.
findings.finding: The eval acceptance criterion uses the phrase 'passing recall + no-fire eval' without a numeric threshold, making it impossible to objectively determine when the criterion is met.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.'
findings.suggested_fix: Example: 'Recall eval: ≥7 of 8 concern-types fired on their target fixtures. No-fire eval: 0 of N no-fire fixtures trigger a finding. Both thresholds enforced in CI.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the eval spec's concrete file path (e.g. src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-performance.eval.yaml), its format (aligned with existing eval specs), the minimum fixture count for recall and no-fire cases, and the numeric pass threshold that satisfies AC2.
findings.finding: The eval spec (second file in Files: 'eval spec + fixtures for the performance overlay') has no concrete path, no format specification, and no analog exists in src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ — making AC2 unverifiable.
findings.location: Plan § Files: 'eval spec + fixtures for the performance overlay (recall + no-fire cases)' / AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Files entry like `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-performance.eval.yaml` and annotate AC2 with e.g. '≥4 recall fixtures all firing, ≥2 no-fire fixtures all suppressed = passing'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'breaks-under-load / worse-with-scale' bright-line test in the overlay prompt itself: specify what reasoning steps constitute it, how it should be encoded in the `evidence` field, and confirm Pass-2 (code-review-verify.md) has a corresponding scoring anchor.
findings.finding: The 'bright-line reasoning' decision protocol ('breaks-under-load / worse-with-scale') is named as the evidence-recording rule for Pass-2 but is never defined — no decision logic, no threshold, no examples exist in the plan or in the current overlay.
findings.location: Plan § What: 'Bright-line reasoning (breaks-under-load / worse-with-scale) recorded as EVIDENCE for Pass-2 to score'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC (e.g., `pytest eval/performance/ -v` for AC2, a grep/ci-lint rule for AC3, and a file-schema check for AC1) so completion can be confirmed deterministically.
findings.finding: No proving command is described for any of the three acceptance criteria; the plan names expected artifacts and properties but provides no concrete command or check that would produce evidence of completion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or eval-spec note specifying that at least a sample of recall and no-fire fixtures must be executed against a live LLM (not offline/canned responses), or document with a named rationale why the offline substitute faithfully represents production LLM behavior.
findings.finding: The eval spec for the performance overlay does not specify whether the evaluation exercises the overlay prompt through a real LLM call or through an offline/canned substitute. For a prompt-engineering change, an offline eval that uses mocked/pre-canned LLM responses never exercises the new prompt content and can go green while the actual overlay is broken in production — the classic proxy-validation anti-pattern.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' / Files — 'eval spec + fixtures for the performance overlay'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: 'Eval includes at least N (e.g., 3) recall and N no-fire fixtures executed live against the target LLM model; offline fixtures may complement but do not replace live validation of the prompt.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Locate or create the artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or comment in code-review-verify.md) that codifies the 'domain rubric belongs in overlays, not the verifier' principle, and cite it in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan references a 'locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier' as a pre-existing settled constraint, but no codebase artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md, design doc, or comment in code-review-verify.md) codifies this principle. It is an assertion about an existing architectural rule with no verifiable grounding.
findings.location: What section — 'per the locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Give the eval spec a concrete repo path (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-performance.eval.yaml`) and add it to the Files list so AC-2 has a verifiable edit target.
findings.finding: The second named file target — 'eval spec + fixtures for the performance overlay (recall + no-fire cases)' — has no concrete path in the plan, and no code-review-performance.eval.yaml (nor any code-review-*.eval.yaml) file exists anywhere in src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/. This is a missing/hallucinated edit target.
findings.location: Files section: 'eval spec + fixtures for the performance overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-performance.eval.yaml` as an explicit named file in the plan's Files section, matching the naming convention of all other packaged eval specs in that directory.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
